questions.question: What is the default value of the bypass dailyBudget config option?
What is the default bypass duration in hours before automatic expiry?
What is the default minimum Node.js version required by the packageJson guard?
How many packages are in this project?
What packages does this project contain?
questions.answer: api, cli, core, doctest, enforcer, gen

questions.question: How many exported symbols does this project have?
What does the core package do?
questions.answer: Append-only audit trail for forge-ts configuration and governance events.  Events are stored as JSON Lines in `.forge-audit.jsonl` at the project root. Each line is a single JSON object — the file is never truncated or overwritten.

questions.question: What does the api package do?
questions.answer: forge-ts/api — OpenAPI spec and API reference generator.  Extracts public SDK types from the symbol graph and generates an OpenAPI 3.2 document and a structured API reference.

questions.question: What does the cli package do?
questions.answer: Forge-ts branded logger built on consola from the UnJS ecosystem.  Provides a singleton logger instance (`forgeLogger`) and a configuration function (`configureLogger`) that respects CLI flags (`--quiet`, `--json`, `--verbose`) and TTY detection.

questions.question: What does the gen package do?
questions.answer: SSG adapter registry for documentation generation.  Provides pluggable adapters for Mintlify, Docusaurus, Nextra, and VitePress that transform forge-ts output into framework-specific documentation files.

questions.question: What does the enforcer package do?
questions.answer: forge-ts/enforcer — TSDoc enforcement linter.  Walks all exported symbols and verifies that each one carries sufficient TSDoc documentation at the required visibility level.

questions.question: What does the doctest package do?
questions.answer: forge-ts/doctest — TSDoc `@example` block extractor and test runner.  Extracts fenced code blocks from `@example` tags in TSDoc comments, generates virtual `node:test` test files, and executes them.

scale.description: Documentation is a perfect reflection of the codebase. Agents can operate with zero source code access.
Documentation is excellent but might miss implementation details found in @remarks.
Useful for API usage, but internal architecture requires source code access.
Documentation is too high-level. Needs deeper @remarks and @example coverage.

rubric.scoring: Each correct answer scores points equal to 100/totalQuestions. Partial credit: 50% for correct concept but wrong specific value.
